title: Evangelion and Gurren Lagann iPhone apps
display_title: Evangelion and Gurren Lagann iPhone apps
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion and Gurren Lagann iPhone apps             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:20 pm [url]http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2010-04-21/gurren-lagann-iphone-app-lands-in-u.s-eva-apps-planned[/url] Maybe now I can put my iPhone to good use.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:31 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Wed Apr 21, 2010 5:08 pm Rei looks depressed in that app. Like, abnormally depressed.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 5:24 pm I would be depressed if I was a 14 year meteorologist.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Apr 22, 2010 6:50 am ANN wrote: One part of the app is a virtual, onscreen Yoko which reacts to touch and vibration by talking and changing expressions.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Thu Apr 22, 2010 7:59 am Looks like Rei the meteorologist is only available in Japan, and rightfully so. That is such a bizarre idea for an app. The Yoko app is good for depressed and sexually repressed teenagers, hence its US release. I don't see any other reason for it.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Apr 22, 2010 4:05 pm Wow, Rei is looking seriously depressed there. That's, like, "Commander Ikari pissed in my socks" levels of depression.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 22, 2010 5:17 pm Heh, Rei does look depressed. I wonder if this weather forecast would lift any thing used from that Misato Katsuragi News Report.... Not surprised that the GL-app is released here but the Eva one isn't, people are going to sell whatever they think is marketable (though I guess they'd feel the need to translate stuff regarding Rei or it would be pointless here if it's a weather forecast for Japan).

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Fri Apr 23, 2010 9:14 am I don't think I mentioned this before -- when I was in Japan last September, I ended up having a long chat over lunch with one of the folks at Tin Machine, who are developing other, different iPhone apps based on Gainax licenses. IIRC they are working on board games. He was interested in knowing what American consumers wanted in localization -- Apple's puritanical standards for apps didn't occur to me at the time. I told him to use subtitles and avoid changes as much as possible.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Apr 23, 2010 10:47 am TIME TO GET ME AN IPHONE! YEE-HAW!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 23, 2010 9:06 pm RAINY DAYS MAKE Rei GLOOMY! drinian: Really?! That would be so awesome if some Eva-based apps made their way to the states. Good job. :3

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Apr 24, 2010 12:05 pm drinian wrote: Apple's puritanical standards for apps

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Apr 24, 2010 1:08 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: RAINY DAYS MAKE Rei GLOOMY!
